","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Select the center for the analysis and the structures or markers to be compared
Select one cell body that you want to use as the center for the analysis
Select one or more process/group of processes, puncta, and/or markers to be analyzed in relation to the cell body
Go to Analyze-> Neuron data -> Spatial and click SHOLL ANALYSIS
Spheres: Choose the starting radius (0µm) and radius increment (10µm) for the spheres

Radius: Each radius corresponds to a shell. The number shown is the position of the shell in µm from the designated center point Intersections: Number of intersections between process and sphere at the given radius Length: Total length in µm of all processes passing through a shell Nodes: Total number of nodes in the shell Endings: Total number of endings in the shell
Trace cell body contour and branching from the cell body in continuous mode
Analyze at least fifteen cells per animal (randomly selected from 3 serial coronal sections/animal) and subsequently average all data for each animal
Analyses: Choose the analyses that you want to include (e.g branching)
creator: Nicola Biagio Mercuri
